assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS.
The intragroup dividend has resulted in a recognised intragroup receivable/payable position relevant to foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to a particular risk affecting profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
